cli.main.help:
Framekit — tracker-ready media workflow toolkit.

Core workflow:
  ren / renamer   Normalize release file names.
  cmk / cleanmkv  Select audio/subtitle tracks and remux clean MKV copies.
  nf / nfo        Build localized tracker-ready NFO files.
  md / metadata   Search and resolve TMDb metadata.
  tor / torrent   Create .torrent files.
  prez            Generate HTML/BBCode presentation files.
  pipe / pipeline Run the full release pipeline.

General commands:
  doctor / doc    Check tools, settings, metadata, templates and diagnostics.
  settings / cfg  Inspect and edit Framekit settings.
  language / lang Show or change the interface language.
  setup / init    Run the guided setup.

Diagnostics:
  --debug               Print tracebacks and write a debug log.
  --log-file <path>     Write structured JSONL logs to a custom path.
  FRAMEKIT_DEBUG=1      Enable debug mode from the environment.
  FRAMEKIT_LOG_FILE=... Write logs from the environment.

Useful examples:
  fk ren <folder>       Preview then confirm renaming.
  fk cmk <folder>       Open the track selector, preview, then confirm remux.
  fk nf <folder> -m     Build an NFO with matching localized TMDb metadata.
  fk pipe <folder> --announce https://tracker/announce
  fk --debug cmk <folder>
  fk cfg get general.locale
  fk lang set fr
  fk doctor

Use '<command> -h' for every option and advanced mode. Add --dry-run where available to preview only.

metadata.setup_help_body:
Metadata setup help

Framekit uses a TMDb read access token for metadata lookups.

Recommended setup:
- Run: framekit setup
- Or prompt a token with: framekit md -t
- Or store a token with: framekit md -T YOUR_TOKEN

Useful commands:
- framekit md -s
- framekit md -c

Supported environment variables:
- FRAMEKIT_TMDB_READ_ACCESS_TOKEN
- FRAMEKIT_METADATA_LANGUAGE

Supported local settings values:
- metadata.tmdb_read_access_token
- metadata.language

Security recommendation:
- Keep credentials local only
- Do not commit them
- Prefer a dedicated personal/project account

setup.custom_metadata_language_body:
Enter a locale code for metadata.

Examples:
- en-US
- fr-FR
- es-419
- de-DE

Type 'back' to return to the previous step.
Type 'quit' to leave setup.
setup.custom_path_body:
Enter a full folder path.

Quotes are optional.
Examples:
- E:\Releases\NFO
- "E:\My Folder\NFO"

Type 'back' to return to the previous step.
Type 'quit' to leave setup.
setup.default_folder_body:
Choose where Framekit should start when no path is passed to the command.

AppData Workspace: managed by Framekit in your user profile.
Framekit Project Workspace: inside your current Framekit project.
Custom Path: anywhere you want.
setup.error.invalid_locale: That does not look like a valid locale code.
setup.error.language_empty: Language cannot be empty. Type 'back' to return or 'quit' to leave setup.
setup.error.path_empty: Path cannot be empty. Type 'back' to return or 'quit' to leave setup.
setup.error.token_empty: Token cannot be empty. Type 'skip', 'back', 'clear' or 'quit'.
setup.import_logo_body:
You can import a text-based logo for templates that support it.

Accepted files: .txt, .nfo, .asc
Type 'back' to return to the previous step.
Type 'quit' to leave setup.
setup.info.no_logos: No logos available. Continuing without logo.
setup.interface_language_body:
This controls the language used by Framekit CLI messages.

Metadata language is configured separately later.
setup.metadata_confirmation_body:
When enabled, Framekit lets you review and confirm the metadata match
before it is used in the NFO.

Recommended: enabled.
setup.metadata_language_body:
This controls the language used when Framekit requests metadata online.

Recommended: English (US) for the most consistent results.
setup.nfo_logo_body:
The logo is optional.

It is printed at the top of templates that support logo rendering.
setup.preferred_template_body:
Built-in templates are simplified here.

Default: lighter output.
Detailed: richer output for tracker-style releases.
setup.success.tmdb_token_valid: TMDb read access token looks valid.
setup.tmdb_token_body:
Framekit uses this token to search movie and episode metadata.

Paste your TMDb Read Access Token here.
Do not paste the TMDb API key.

Type 'skip' to continue without changing this value.
Type 'back' to return to the previous step.
Type 'clear' to remove an existing token.
Type 'quit' to leave setup.
setup.warning.logo_not_resolved: Could not resolve selected logo. Continuing without logo.
setup.welcome_body:
Welcome to Framekit guided setup.

This setup will guide you through default folders, metadata, and NFO preferences.
Nothing is saved until the final confirmation screen.
